expectations.text: Uses exchange_calendars library (xcals) rather than hand-rolling hour-range checks
expectations.evidence: Line 24: 'import exchange_calendars as xcals'. Full SessionDetector class wraps xcals.get_calendar() with MIC codes (XNYS, XLON, XTKS).

expectations.text: Uses vectorized approach (pre-computed sets + .isin(), or equivalent) rather than per-row .apply()
expectations.evidence: Lines 102-144: _precompute_trading_minutes() builds Set[pd.Timestamp] per exchange. Line 218: 'df[col_name] = ts.isin(trading_minutes[key])' — vectorized .isin().

expectations.text: Correctly handles Tokyo lunch break (11:30-12:30 JST) — either via is_open_on_minute() or explicit exclusion
expectations.evidence: Line 134: 'if calendar.is_open_on_minute(current)' during pre-computation. Lunch break excluded by exchange_calendars automatically. Verified in demo at line 316.

expectations.text: DST is handled implicitly via IANA timezones (not manual UTC offset tables)
expectations.evidence: No manual DST logic anywhere. Line 39: ExchangeConfig stores IANA timezone. exchange_calendars handles DST internally.

expectations.text: Output is a complete, runnable Python script (not pseudocode or partial snippets)
expectations.evidence: 326 lines, includes imports, classes, convenience function, and __main__ demo with self-test assertions. Fully runnable.
